description: PostHog feature flag `ticket-bento-layout` gates visibility of the Grid|Entry layout toggle on the MSP ticket detail screen
Flag off (or toggle set to Entry) renders the existing TicketDetails layout with no behavioral or visual change and no toggle when flag is off
Toggle set to Grid renders new TicketBentoLayout component from the same consolidated ticket payload (no duplicate fetch)
Bento grid shell: 12-column responsive grid with hero band (span 12), left rail (span 3), timeline (span 6), right rail (span 3)
Responsive collapse at ~1024-1279px: rails re-span and right rail flows under left rail
Responsive collapse below 1024px: single column ordered hero, timeline, SLA clocks, checklist, remaining tiles; no horizontal scroll
Shared bento tile primitive (header, title, optional action slot, body) built on ContentCard styling with theme tokens
Tile-level error state: a tile whose data errors shows a visible error message inside the tile (fail fast, no blank cards)
Hero band shows ticket number (mono), inline-editable title, and created/updated metadata line
Hero band status control: editable select styled as chip, wired to existing dirty-field tracking
Hero band priority control: editable select with priority color dot
Hero band board control: editable select
Hero band assignee control: editable agent picker with avatar
Hero band due date control: editable date/time picker
Hero band response-state chip with copy 'Waiting on us' / 'Waiting on client' / 'No reply needed', editable via existing response-state options
Hero band tags row: view, add, and remove tags using the existing tag manager
Hero band resolution-SLA countdown slab (compact) sourced from the same computation as the SLA clocks tile
Hero band actions: Create task and Link to task preserved (existing handlers/render props)
Hero band action: Delete ticket with existing confirmation flow
Hero band action: Save changes button reflecting dirty state (disabled when clean), plus Cancel to revert
deferralReason: Superseded by design: hero controls persist immediately via handleSelectChange (decision 2026-07-03); batch Save/Cancel exists in the All fields drawer via TicketInfo.

description: Prev/next ticket navigation and back-to-tickets link preserved in the top bar/hero
Ticket origin badge (e.g. created via inbound email) preserved in hero band
Presence bar (live viewers) preserved on the new layout
'More details' drawer from hero exposes category and subcategory pickers
'More details' drawer exposes ITIL impact/urgency fields (when board is ITIL-enabled)
'More details' drawer exposes location picker
'More details' drawer exposes contact override picker
'More details' drawer fields participate in the same dirty-field/batch-save pipeline as hero fields
Email notification logs drawer remains reachable from the new layout
Escalation state (escalated flag/level) visible in hero when set
Timeline data: extend unified timeline to interleave time entries (work_item_id + work_item_type='TICKET') as 'time' lane entries
Timeline data: interleave RMM alerts (rmm_alerts.ticket_id) as 'alert' lane entries with severity, device name, occurrence count
Timeline data: comments carry lane (client reply vs internal note vs resolution) derived from is_internal/is_resolution
Timeline data: audit rows keep curated diff summaries (existing curatedTicketDiff) for system lane display
Timeline action returns entries sorted by occurrence time with stable ordering for same-timestamp entries
Timeline action remains withAuth + ticket:read gated and hard-blocked for client-type users
Timeline action is tenant-scoped in every new query (tenant column in joins/filters)
New read action: schedule entries for ticket (schedule_entries by work_item_id/work_item_type), upcoming first
New read action: interactions for ticket (interactions.ticket_id) with type, duration, date, most recent first
New read action: billing rollup for ticket (billable vs non-billable minutes, invoiced flag, active contract name for the client)
New tile reads load without blocking first paint of hero + timeline (parallel or per-tile fetch)
Consolidated fetch not duplicated: tiles reuse payload fields already returned by getConsolidatedTicketData where available
Timeline tile renders inline in the center column (replaces drawer-only access when flag on)
Timeline header copy: title 'Timeline', subtitle 'Replies, time, and system changes in one place'
Lane filter pills: Everything / Replies / Time / System / Alerts with live counts
Filter pills toggle lanes client-side without refetch and are buttons with aria-pressed
Day-break separators between calendar days, labeled with plain dates and 'Today'
Client reply rendering: author, timestamp, bubble treatment, client-lane accent
Internal note rendering: visually distinct treatment with 'Internal' label
Resolution comments render with a 'Resolution' marker
Time entry rendering: actor, duration badge, notes line
System event rendering: compact single-line with curated change summary and actor
Alert rendering: warning treatment with alert type, device, occurrence count
Sort toggle oldest-first/newest-first with choice persisted per user; composer stays at bottom in oldest-first (default) order
Comment reactions preserved on timeline comment entries
Comment edit/delete affordances preserved for permitted users on timeline comment entries
Reply composer at timeline bottom with client/internal/resolution segment control
Composer placeholder copy: 'Reply to {contact first name}…' with fallback 'Write a reply'
Composer uses the existing rich-text editor and addTicketComment save path; new comment appears in stream after save
Composer attachment affordance preserved (matches current comment attachment behavior)
Timeline empty state: 'Nothing yet. The ticket was opened {date}.'
Timeline keyboard navigation: entries and controls reachable and operable by keyboard
Request tile: description content (rich text render) with edit affordance wired to existing description save
Request tile shows origin line (source/channel) and created-by
Contact tile: contact name, email, phone, location from consolidated payload
Contact tile: opens existing ContactDetailsView drawer (injected render prop preserved)
Contact tile: client name opens existing ClientQuickView (injected render prop preserved)
Assets tile: AssociatedAssets relocated into left rail as a tile (injected node preserved)
Next visit tile: upcoming schedule entries for this ticket with date block, title, assignee, time range
Next visit tile shows most recent past entry dimmed when no upcoming entries exist alongside data
Next visit empty state: 'Nothing scheduled' with a schedule action linking to dispatch/scheduling
Calls and emails tile: interactions for this ticket with type icon, title, date
Calls and emails tile links to the full interactions view for the ticket/client
Calls and emails empty state: 'No calls or emails logged'
Request tile handles long descriptions with clamped preview and expand
Left rail tiles render in defined order: Request, Contact, Assets, Next visit, Calls and emails
Contact tile empty state when ticket has no contact: shows client only with add-contact affordance
Assets tile add/link asset affordance preserved from current AssociatedAssets
SLA clocks tile: response clock state (met in Xm / Xm left / overdue by X / paused) from sla_* columns
SLA clocks tile: resolution clock with progress ring/bar showing percent of window elapsed
SLA clocks tile shows policy name chip and pause note when status pauses the clock
SLA clocks empty state: 'No SLA policy applies'
SLA computation shared between hero slab and SLA tile (single source, reusing existing sla status logic)
Checklist tile: items with completion checkboxes, progress count, add-item action (existing checklist actions)
Checklist tile: progress bar reflecting completed/total
Time logged tile: total hours headline and entry count ('11h across 4 entries')
Time logged tile: per-day mini bar chart of logged time
Time logged tile: session timer controls (start/pause/stop + elapsed display) preserved via useTicketTimeTracking
Time logged tile: 'Add time entry' opens the existing time entry dialog flow
Time logged tile: interval management (tracked intervals) reachable via existing injected IntervalManagement
Billing tile: billable vs non-billable hours and invoiced state ('Not invoiced yet')
Billing tile: governing contract name with 'Covered by contract' line when contract link resolves
Billing tile empty state: 'No time logged yet'; tile hidden when billing permission absent
Team and watchers tile: agent team row (assignee + additional agents) with existing add/remove and schedule drawer
Team and watchers tile: watchers row with existing watch-list add/remove (contact/internal/email tabs preserved)
Materials tile: existing TicketMaterialsCard relocated, AlgaDesk-gated
Customer feedback tile: existing survey summary card relocated
Documents tile: existing documents section relocated (new/upload/link actions preserved)
Right rail tiles render in defined order: SLA clocks, Checklist, Time logged, Billing, Team and watchers, Materials, Customer feedback, Documents
Knowledge base tile (stretch): suggested articles from naive title/tag match, labeled 'Suggested'
deferralReason: Stretch feature deferred by resolved product decision (PRD Open Question 4).

description: Per-tile section ids stable and unique for reflection UI (every interactive element has an id)
All tiles styled with CSS variable tokens and dark: variants; both themes verified
Dirty-field tracking spans hero + more-details drawer and merges into one batch save (parity with handleBatchSaveChanges)
deferralReason: Superseded by design: hero uses immediate-save semantics, not batch; drawer TicketInfo retains batch save.

description: Unsaved-changes guard (UnsavedChangesProvider) active across all editable bento surfaces
deferralReason: Superseded by design: immediate-save hero fields create no unsaved state; the All fields drawer (TicketInfo) retains the unsaved-changes guard.

description: Live-edit conflict props (liveFieldConflicts/liveEditingUsers/liveHighlightedFields) wired to hero and drawer fields
Save updates propagate to timeline and tiles via existing router.refresh flow
TicketDetails state extraction: bento layout consumes the same state/handlers without forking write logic (single source for save handlers)
AlgaDesk hideSlaStatus hides SLA tile and hero SLA slab; grid re-flows without gaps
AlgaDesk hideTimeEntry hides Time logged tile and timer controls; grid re-flows
AlgaDesk hideMaterials hides Materials tile; grid re-flows
Client-portal ticket view render path untouched (no imports from new bento modules)
Activity drawer entry point remains functional while flag is in rollout
All UI copy strings match the PRD house-voice table (audited against the table)
Bento layout renders correctly for a minimal ticket (no contact, no SLA, no entries) using designed empty states everywhere
Bento layout renders correctly for a data-rich ticket (fixture TIC1001-scale: comments, time, checklist, assets, alerts)
Segmented control labeled 'Grid | Entry' in the ticket screen header switches layouts without a full page reload
Layout choice (Grid vs Entry) persists per user and applies on subsequent ticket loads
Default layout is Entry for users who have never used the toggle
